overview.title: Project Overview
overview.subtitle: Event-Driven Order Management System
overview.description: Distributed e-commerce platform that manages the complete purchase order lifecycle with eventual consistency guarantees in a microservices environment.

problemItems.4: Complete auditability of the order system

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.internalComponentsSubtitle: Patterns: Outbox Pattern + Consistent Hashing + Partitions

limitations.title: Limitations & Non-Goals
limitations.notImplementedTitle: What the System Does NOT Do

orderService.role: Order management, Event Sourcing, Outbox Worker

eventSourcing.description: Stores state as a sequence of events instead of current state
eventSourcing.benefits: Complete audit trail, State replay, Temporal debugging

outboxPattern.description: Reliably publishes events using outbox table
outboxPattern.benefits: Delivery guarantee, No external CDC, Transactional

sagaChoreography.description: Distributed orchestration through messages between services
sagaChoreography.benefits: Total decoupling, Horizontal scalability, Fault isolation

deduplication.description: Prevention of duplicate processing with Redis

consistentHashing.description: Uniform distribution of events between workers

eventSourcing.why: Complete auditability, event replay, detailed state debugging
eventSourcing.tradeoff: Implementation complexity, learning curve, requires projections for efficient reads

outboxPattern.why: Reliable event publishing without external CDC, simple to implement
outboxPattern.tradeoff: 5s latency (polling), not real-time, requires additional workers

sagaChoreography.why: Total decoupling between services, horizontal scalability
sagaChoreography.tradeoff: Hard to trace, complex debugging, risk of infinite loops

hexagonalArchitecture.why: Testability, framework independence, ease to change technology
hexagonalArchitecture.tradeoff: Additional boilerplate, learning curve, more files

postgresqlPerService.why: Fault isolation, team independence, microservices compliance
postgresqlPerService.tradeoff: Complex eventual consistency, impossible distributed joins, more operational

authentication.detail: No JWT, API keys, or OAuth observed

authorization.detail: Any client can query any order

auth.detail: Any client can access any resource

singleNode.detail: Single point of failure in persistence

footer.text: Distributed Commerce Platform - Event-Driven Architecture Documentation
